dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3728 IS: Long-Distance Corridor Relief Enhancement Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Sheehy (for himself and Mr. Merkley) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation

form.official-title: To remove the requirement that the Secretary of Transportation consider the committed or anticipated non-Federal funding for long distance intercity passenger rail routes under the Corridor Identification and Development Program.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Long-Distance Corridor Relief Enhancement Act.

section.header: Modification of consideration requirement of non-Federal funding for corridor selection
section.text: Section 25101(c) of title 49, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (1) through (14) as subparagraphs (A) through (N), respectively, and indenting such subparagraphs, as so redesignated, 2 ems to the right;
in the matter preceding subparagraph (A), as so redesignated, by striking In selecting and inserting the following:
in subparagraph (F), as so redesignated, by striking committed or and by inserting except as provided in paragraph (2), committed or; and
by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: For the purposes of this subsection, the Secretary shall not require or consider committed or anticipated non-Federal funding for any part of the program described in subsection (a) for any intercity passenger rail corridor on a long-distance route.
The exception provided in subparagraph (A) shall apply to any long-distance route corridor accepted into the program on or after October 1, 2023.
